subject: DevOps
title: The Build Step
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Build Step.

hook.text: Code sits in a repo as text files. The build step transforms it into something a machine can run.

buildup.text: Compiling, bundling, transpiling, linking — the build produces an artifact: a JAR, binary, or container image.

discovery.text: Builds must be deterministic. Same input, same output, every time. No 'it works on my machine' excuses.

twist.text: Build caching speeds things up. Only rebuild what changed. A cached build takes seconds, not minutes.

climax.text: If the build fails, the pipeline stops. A failing build is the first signal that something is wrong.

punchline.text: Build once, produce an artifact. Same input, same output.

quiz.question: What is a build artifact?
options: The runnable output of the build step like a binary or container image
The source code stored in Git
